cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. First, I need to go back to the text and see what's mentioned. The author was a shy, introverted student who was the only nonwhite student at her school. She was into reading and writing, which was different from her classmates who didn't like novels because they weren't "real." Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest and offered her reading suggestions. The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something "a little different." The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recitation, the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for picking a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. The author felt confident for the first time. So, why did the teacher ask her to do this? Let's look at the options: A) To test memory. The passage isn't about memorizing; it's about reciting a meaningful part. The teacher probably didn't care about her memory skills here. B) To earn respect. While the class applauded, the main reason might not be about earning respect. The author was shy and maybe didn't have respect from others, but the teacher's role here seems more supportive than trying to make her "earn" something from peers. C) To deepen understanding of the book. Choosing a passage to recite might help with understanding, but the teacher's suggestion was "a little different" from a standard book report. The focus was on selecting and delivering a passage, which could be to help her connect more with the material. However, the outcome was more about the author's confidence. D) To build relationships. The teacher was helping her come out of her shell. The author was very shy, and by doing the recitation, she gained confidence. The teacher's actions were aimed at making her feel less shy and more confident. This suggests building relationships with others or helping her socialize. But wait, the answer choices: C is about understanding the book, D is about relationships. The text says Ms. Leibfried noticed the author's interest and made her feel less
